input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #58411: sessions_spawn lacks --bind here equivalent — agent cannot bind ACP session to existing Discord thread
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 58411
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/58411
- Title: sessions_spawn lacks --bind here equivalent — agent cannot bind ACP session to existing Discord thread
- State: OPEN
- Author: brew-agent
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, impact:message-loss, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

When a user is in an existing Discord thread and asks the agent to start a coding session, the correct UX is `/acp spawn <agent> --bind here` — which attaches the ACP session to the current thread in place.

However, this slash command can only be run by a human. If the user asks the **agent** to spawn the session, the agent has no programmatic equivalent. `sessions_spawn` only supports `thread: true`, which always **creates a new thread** — which is the wrong behavior here.

## Use case

1. User and agent are having a discussion in an existing Discord thread
2. User says: _"Start a Codex session on this"_
3. Expected: agent binds an ACP session to the current thread — conversation continues in place
4. Actual: agent has to ask the user to manually run `/acp spawn <agent> --bind here`, breaking the flow

## Proposed solution

Add a `bindTo: "current"` option to `sessions_spawn` that maps to `--bind here` semantics — attaches the ACP session to the current thread without creating a new one:

```json
{
  "runtime": "acp",
  "mode": "session",
  "bindTo": "current",
  "task": "..."
}
```

## Related

- #41004 — same slash command vs `sessions_spawn` asymmetry on Telegram
```

This PR fixes two proxy-related reliability issues for Discord gateway connections: it bootstraps a global `EnvHttpProxyAgent` so Node's `fetch()` honours `https_proxy`/`HTTP_PROXY` env vars, and disables `keepAlive` on all `ProxyAgent` / `EnvHttpProxyAgent` instances to prevent `ECONNRESET` from local proxies (Clash, Surge, etc.) that close HTTP CONNECT tunnels after a single request. - `run.ts`: adds `ensureGlobalUndiciEnvProxyDispatcher()` before `ensureGlobalUndiciStreamTimeouts()` so that the timeout options wrap the already-active `EnvHttpProxyAgent` (ordering comment is clear and correct). - `undici-global-dispatcher.ts`: `keepAlive: false` is added to both the initial proxy bootstrap and the timeout-wrapped `EnvHttpProxyAgent`. Body:
```markdown
# [Feature] Add MiMo-V2.5 to Xiaomi catalog + automatic multimodal routing when DeepSeek V4-Pro is primary model

# [Feature] Add MiMo-V2.5 to Xiaomi catalog + automatic multimodal routing when DeepSeek V4-Pro is primary model

## Summary

Two related asks in one issue:

1. **Add `xiaomi/mimo-v2.5` to the built-in Xiaomi provider catalog** — it was released on April 22 2026 and supersedes `mimo-v2-omni` across every multimodal benchmark.
2. **Auto-route to a multimodal model when the primary model is text-only and the incoming message contains image / video / audio attachments** — so users running DeepSeek V4-Pro as their default never have to manually switch models.

---

## Why MiMo-V2.5 instead of MiMo-V2-Omni

| Property | `mimo-v2-omni` | `mimo-v2.5` |
|---|---|---|
| Parameters | — | 310B total / 15B active (sparse MoE) |
| Modalities | text, image | text, image, video, audio |
| Context window | 262,144 | 1,048,576 |
| Max output | 32,000 | 131,072 |
| Reasoning | ✅ | ✅ |
| Input price | — | $0.40 / 1M tokens |
| Output price | — | $2.00 / 1M tokens |
| API model id | `mimo-v2-omni` | `mimo-v2.5` |
| Base URL | `https://api.xiaomimimo.com/v1` | same |

MiMo-V2.5 trains image, video, and audio encoders jointly from the start — not adapter-bolted-on. It also beats MiMo-V2-Omni on Video-MME (87.7) and MMMU-Pro (77.9), and runs at roughly half the cost of MiMo-V2-Pro.

---

## Requested catalog entry

Add to `src/providers/xiaomi/catalog.ts` (or equivalent):

```ts
{
 id: "mimo-v2.5",
 name: "Xiaomi MiMo V2.5",
 reasoning: true,
 input: ["text", "image", "video", "audio"],
 cost: { input: 0.40, output: 2.00, cacheRead: 0.04, cacheWrite: 0 },
 contextWindow: 1_048_576,
 maxTokens: 131_072,
},
```

> The existing `mimo-v2-omni` entry should be kept for backward compat but marked as superseded in docs.

---

## Requested behavior: automatic multimodal routing

### Problem

When a user sets `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` (or any text-only model) as primary:

```jsonc
// openclaw.config.json
{
 "agents": {
 "defaults": {
 "model": { "primary": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro" }
 }
 }
}
```

…and then attaches an image, video, or audio file to a message, OpenClaw currently either errors out or strips the attachment silently.

### Proposed behavior

OpenClaw should inspect the `input` capability array of the resolved primary model before dispatching. If the message contains attachment types the primary model does not declare support for, it should automatically re-route

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

input.github_context:
GitHub item:

- Type: issue

- Title: [Feature]: doctor api/extendability
- State: OPEN
- Author: pranitl
- Labels: enhancement, P3, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:security, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
### Summary

extensibility on the doctors checks we run so that we can do custom environment specific checks to identify issues with the setup before hand and have it protentially fixed as well by a --fix command.

### Problem to solve

was looking to get a doctor extensibility thing so i can add custom checks on the openclaw doctors command and it gets supported/maintained for future releases and that the npm upgrade doesn’t override it.

for context, i’m running 2 gateways on the same VM using one codex backend provider thing so that a family member can have their own claw instance using my openai sub and they don’t have to maintain a vm but still in a way where we both get logical separation.

this becomes quite fragile for and I just got hit with errors like “access not configured” in what should have been the main/root DM because one profile drifted into Telegram pairing mode because we allow listed our telegram ids on each instance.  that kind of config conflict should be caught before it breaks prod and i had codex write out a local script and even had it update the doctor checks itself but again, when we npm upgrade, that’ll get wiped out. some extensibility on the openclaw setup overall and maybe adding in some checks could be useful? I know a lot of people complain they have to have codex fix openclaw setups so given their env, this could allow people to have more automated workflows/run books for diagnosis/fixes.

### Proposed solution

be able to add in a plugin/extensibility type of architecture to the doctor checks so that we can have it do check/fixes in a customized manner beyond baseline. 

### Alternatives considered

I tried having codex build out a custom check within the src code of the doctor itself but it would be override. post upgrade. plugin type arch could be very helpful to persist the custom env specific checks. 

### Impact

- Not sure who’s affected besides me but i’d imagine others who are running multiple claw instances with a shared backend and same model provider also can be impacted.
- not blocking but a nuisance which i guess i can manage but id imagine the community can also use the doctor checks to have even more functionality for also how anything that uses openclaw as a backend can be extended for migration safety with ability to add custom doctor extensions.
- edge case/frequent depending on setup but again with this feature i’d image a lot of ppl could benefit depending on how they use the checks/self healing play

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

- mayank6136 at 2026-04-27T05:48:51Z:
Saw this issue and dug into the doctor surface — the internal architecture already supports pluggable checks (`src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`), so the gap is just the public plugin-SDK API + `--fix` plumbing. Happy to take this on if maintainers green-light a path.

@pranitl — your specific multi-gateway scenario (family-member separation, Telegram-allowlist drift between profiles) is also a great motivating example, and the proposal below would let you ship that exact check as either a small bundled-in default or a sidecar plugin you maintain.

**What's already there (no work needed):**
- `DoctorHealthContribution` model in `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts` — built-in checks already follow a contribution shape (`{id, kind: "core", surface: "health", run}`).
- `src/plugin-sdk/runtime-doctor.ts` exists as a re-export façade, but it currently exposes only a few internal helpers (`detectPluginInstallPathIssue`, etc.) — no "register your own check" API.

**Preferred fix path:**

1. **Extend the contribution model** to accept `kind: "plugin"` alongside `kind: "core"`, with a `pluginId` field for source attribution. Same `run` signature; existing built-ins unaffected.
2. **Add `fix?: (ctx) => Promise<FixResult>`** to the contribution shape. `FixResult` = `{ applied: boolean; message: string; reversible?: boolean }`. Optional — checks without a fix just report.
3. **New public plugin SDK API** in `src/plugin-sdk/runtime-docto

input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 71487
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/71487
- Title: Web UI: add a clear TTS toggle and default voice picker in Settings
- State: OPEN
- Author: wen0531
- Labels: P3, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, clawsweeper:linked-pr-open, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Problem
Right now TTS is configurable, but the UX is much more command/config driven than it should be.

In practice, a user can enable TTS with commands like:
- `openclaw infer tts enable`
- `openclaw infer tts disable`
- `openclaw infer tts set-provider --provider microsoft`

and preferences are written to `~/.openclaw/settings/tts.json`.

That works technically, but from the Control UI / webchat perspective, there is no obvious visible "switch" for:
- turning TTS on/off
- seeing whether it is currently enabled
- selecting a default provider
- selecting a default voice

This creates a mismatch between capability and discoverability.

## Request
Please add a first-class TTS section in the Control UI Settings (or another obvious UI surface) with:

1. **A visible TTS enable/disable toggle**
   - reflect current state
   - persist to the same underlying preferences

2. **Default provider selector**
   - show available/configured providers
   - indicate unavailable/unconfigured providers clearly

3. **Default voice selector**
   - filter by selected provider
   - especially important for providers like Microsoft/Google/MiniMax with many voices

4. **Optional test/play button**
   - generate a short sample line with the currently selected provider/voice
   - helps users verify the setup without using CLI commands

## Why this matters
TTS already exists and can be configured, but users reasonably expect an actual UI switch for a feature this visible. Right now the product feels like it has TTS "under the hood" rather than as a polished surfaced capability.

## Concrete example
A user wanted to:
- enable TTS
- set default voice to `zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`

This ended up being done through CLI/config instead of a simple Settings toggle + voice dropdown.

## Suggested UX copy
- **Text-to-Speech**
- Toggle: "Read replies aloud when supported"
- Provider: dropdown
- Voice: dropdown
- Test button: "Play sample"

Thanks — this feels like a pretty high-leverage polish fix.

```

input.github_context:

- State: OPEN
- Author: app/kai-the-man
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, impact:session-state, impact:security, issue-rating: 🌊 off-meta tidepool

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Allow OpenClaw skills to be injected into ACP (Codex/Pi/OpenCode/Gemini) session contexts when spawning agents.

## Problem

Currently:
- Skills exist in `~/clawd/skills/` and augment Kai's capabilities
- ACP agents spawn as independent processes with no access to these skills
- When delegating to Codex/Pi for specialized tasks, I must manually copy relevant skill content into the task prompt

## Proposed Solution

Add a mechanism to optionally include rendered skill content when calling `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")`. Options:

1. **Automatic relevance detection**: Analyze task and include relevant skills
2. **Explicit skill list**: Allow `skills: ["k8s", "github"]` parameter in spawn call
3. **Default skill set**: Configure a default set of skills to always inject

## Example Usage

```json
{
  "runtime": "acp",
  "agentId": "codex",
  "skills": ["k8s", "github"],  // or "auto"
  "task": "Deploy the app to k8s-cluster-1"
}
```

## Expected Behavior

- Relevant skill content (SKILL.md, context rules) gets rendered into the initial task prompt
- ACP agent receives the skill context as part of its system/user prompt
- Maintains isolation (skills don't grant OpenClaw-only tools)

## Version

OpenClaw 2026.3.8 (3caab92)

## Related

- Skill location: `~/clawd/skills/`
- ACP runtime: `sessions_spawn` with `runtime: "acp"`
```

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

- Forward `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, and `seed` from inbound `POST /v1/chat/completions` through `streamParams` → runtime stream wrapper → OpenAI transport, so client-supplied sampling parameters reach the upstream provider.
- Extend `AgentStreamParams` with `frequencyPenalty`, `presencePenalty`, and `seed` fields. Forward them through `createStreamFnWithExtraParams` (extra-params layer) and `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` (transport layer) to the provider request payload.
- Add gateway-side validation for the new parameters in `validateOpenAiSamplingParams`: penalty params constrained to `[-2.0, 2.0]`, `seed` must be an integer — matching the existing `temperature`/`top_p` validation contract.
- Resolve aliased param keys with camelCase (runtime/request) priority over snake_case (config) so per-request gateway overrides take precedence over configured model defaults.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.sampling.test.ts` — 8/8 (existing sampling tests, no regressions)
- `pnpm test src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` — 168/168 (no regressions)
- `pnpm test src/gateway/openai-http.usage.test.ts src/gateway/openai-http.image-budget.test.ts` — 7/7 (no regressions)
- `pnpm check:changed` — clean (locally)
- `codex review` — "No discrete correctness issues were identified in the provided diff."
- Fork CI (`Lellansin/openclaw`, run [26092092169](https://github.com/Lellansin/openclaw/actions/runs/26092092169)) — 54/54 jobs passed

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI-compatible Gateway HTTP requests now forward client-supplied `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, and `seed` from `POST /v1/chat/completions` through `streamParams` to the upstream OpenAI-compatible provider. Invalid values are rejected at the gateway boundary with deterministic OpenAI-compatible 400 errors.
- Real environment tested: Local dev gateway on loopback port 18790, built from commit `044a61060a` with `pnpm build`. Runtime Node v24.14.1, CLI/gateway version 2026.5.19. Default agent model `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`. Gateway token and provider key read from local OpenClaw config.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm build`, then stopped the managed gateway service, started a dev gateway on port 18790, and posted 7 authenticated local requests to `http://127.0.0.1:18790/v1/chat/completions`: a happy-path request, three invalid-value probes (out-of-range `

input.github_context:

- State: OPEN
- Author: kAIborg24
- Labels: bug, bug:behavior

Body:
```markdown
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP sessions fail even though direct ACPX to Codex succeeds. The managed path prepares an isolated Codex home without Codex auth, and after auth is bridged locally it still sends an unsupported timeout config option that Codex ACP rejects.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use OpenClaw 2026.4.26 with ACP enabled and Codex available as an ACP agent.
2. Verify direct ACPX to Codex can complete a trivial prompt using the normal Codex home.
3. Run direct ACPX with an isolated CODEX_HOME that contains only an OpenClaw-style generated config file; observe that Codex reports Authentication required.
4. Add only a local auth.json bridge/symlink into that isolated Codex home and rerun direct ACPX; observe that Codex succeeds.
5. Run an OpenClaw-managed ACP Codex session with a trivial prompt.

### Expected behavior

OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP should either reuse/bridge existing Codex authentication safely or provide a documented setup flow for the isolated Codex home, then run the prompt without sending unsupported generic config options.


### Actual behavior

OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP initially fails because the isolated Codex home has no authentication. After adding a local auth bridge, the managed session advances but fails before the prompt with: Agent rejected session/set_config_option for "timeout"="120": Invalid params (ACP -32602).


### OpenClaw version

2026.4.26 (be8c246)

### Operating system

Ubuntu 26.04 LTS

### Install method

npm global install

### Model

Codex ACP default / OpenClaw-managed ACP Codex agent

### Provider / routing chain

OpenClaw managed ACP runtime -> ACPX -> Codex ACP adapter

### Additional provider/model setup details

Direct ACPX to Codex works on the same machine and authentication. The failure appears specific to OpenClaw-managed ACP session preparation/config handoff.


### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Direct ACPX with normal Codex home succeeds.
Direct ACPX with isolated Codex home lacking auth fails with: Authentication required.
Direct ACPX with isolated Codex home plus only auth.json bridge succeeds.
OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP then fails with: Agent rejected session/set_config_option for "timeout"="120": Invalid params (ACP -32602).
Removing the user-configured timeout value does not stop timeout=120 from being sent because the ACPX plugin schema applies timeoutSeconds: 120 as a 

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

The open explicit-home override PR is the current candidate lane, but it still needs maintainer/security review before this issue can close.

Reproducibility: yes. Source and dependency contracts show current main still launches Codex ACP with an isolated CODEX_HOME that lacks auth while the pinned adapter reads auth from that resolved home; the issue comments provide matching real-world Ubuntu and macOS symptoms.

**Next step**
Maintainer/security review should choose the Codex ACP auth boundary and either rework or close https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77006; this is not a safe automatic fix lane while the credential contract is unsettled.

**Security**
Needs attention: The remaining work changes Codex credential boundaries and should not be automated without maintainer/security review.


<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Keep the timeout suppression and land a reviewed ACPX auth path, likely an explicit Codex-home override or documented isolated-home login flow, without default credential materialization into OpenClaw state.

Do we have a high-co

input.ds4_description: OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP sessions fail due to isolated CODEX_HOME lacking auth bridge and unsupported timeout config forwarding. The bug involves three layers: the ACPX plugin schema sends timeoutSeconds=120 as an unsupported ACP config option (rejected with -32602), the managed wrapper forces an isolated Codex home without reusing existing auth, and the credential boundary for Codex ACP auth remains unresolved. Direct ACPX to Codex succeeds with a normal or bridged auth home. The fix requires suppressing the unsupported timeout and landing a reviewed ACPX auth path.
ds4_caveats: Issue is still open; the timeout suppression fix may already be in main but auth half remains unresolved pending maintainer/security review of PR #77006.

This fallback provides a bounded, safe downgrade path and reduces stall risk without broad behavior changes.\n\n## Validation\nTargeted tests passed:\n- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.loop.test.ts\n- src/agents/failover-error.test.ts\n- src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts\n- src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts\n\n## Scope & safety\n- Single-purpose micro-fix (no unrelated files in this commit).\n- No secrets/keys included in committed patch (pattern scan clean).\n- AI-assisted PR. ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-12T20:57:45Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR hardens the context-overflow recovery loop in the embedded PI runner by adding one suppress-hook retry (suppressing external memory/prompt-hook injections) as a last-resort fallback before giving up, and normalises `model_context_window_exceeded` / `context_window_exceeded` stop-reason strings into the existing overflow handling path across error formatting, failover classification, and user-facing text sanitisation. - **Suppress-hook retry** (`run.ts`, `attempt.ts`, `types.ts`): adds a single bounded retry with `suppressPromptHookContext: true` after compaction and tool-truncation recovery both fail. Correctly guarded by `!isCompactionFailure` to preserve `compaction_failure` semantics. - **Stop-reason normalisation** (`errors.ts`, `failover-matches.ts`): extends `CONTEXT_OVERFLOW_ERROR_HEAD_RE` and the failover `timeout` pattern to recognise raw stop-reason strings; `formatRawAssistantErrorForUi` now returns friendly copy for these variants early. - **Flag persistence concern**: `promptHookContextSuppressedForOverflow` is set to `true` and never reset. Within a single `runEmbeddedPiAgent` call, all subsequent `runEmbeddedAttempt` invocations (i.e. every follow-up prompt after tool calls) will also run with hooks suppressed — even if the suppress retry succeeded and the overflow is resolved. For long tool-call chains this silently disables memory/hook context for all remaining turns. input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #63229: Bug: Gateway falsely marks healthy local vLLM endpoints as timed out/overloaded, causing 1–23 min fallback cascades
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 63229
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/63229
- Title: Bug: Gateway falsely marks healthy local vLLM endpoints as timed out/overloaded, causing 1–23 min fallback cascades
- State: OPEN
- Author: clawdia-lobster
- Labels: P1, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-info, impact:auth-provider, impact:crash-loop, issue-rating: 🦐 gold shrimp

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

The gateway's model-fallback/routing subsystem incorrectly marks healthy, responsive local vLLM endpoints as "timed out" or "overloaded", causing cascading fallback chains that take **1–23 minutes** to resolve. The endpoints themselves respond in **0.27–0.93s** when tested directly via curl.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: 2026.4.5 (container, Linux 6.12.63 x64)
- Gateway: loopback bind, port 18789
- Local vLLM endpoints:
  - `vllm-8001` (gemma4, 27B) on jupiter.wg.local:8001 — dedicated GPU
  - `vllm-7002` (qwen3.5-27b) on jupiter.wg.local:7002 — dedicated GPU
- Remote providers: Novita (GLM-5, Kimi), DeepInfra (Kimi), Anthropic (Sonnet)
- Config: `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds: 1200`, `agents.defaults.llm.idleTimeoutSeconds: 300`

## Observed Behaviour

### 1. Endpoints are fast (direct curl, concurrent)

Both GPUs idle, tested concurrently:

| Endpoint | Avg latency (5 reqs) |
|----------|---------------------|
| vllm-8001/gemma4 | **0.28s** |
| vllm-7002/qwen3.5-27b | **0.91s** |

### 2. Gateway marks them as timed out or overloaded

From gateway logs, model fallback decisions for today:

**Failure reasons:**
- `timeout`: 17 occurrences
- `unknown`: 8
- `overloaded`: 2

**Error previews:**
- `LLM request timed out.`: 12
- `Gateway is draining for restart; new tasks are not accepted`: 8
- `cron: job execution timed out`: 4
- `Live session model switch requested: <model>`: 2
- `Request was aborted.`: 1

### 3. Fallback chains take minutes

Example fallback chains from today's logs:

| Run ID | Chain | Total time |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| `7c914aae` | qwen→timeout → Kimi→timeout → gemma→timeout → **Sonnet✓** | **23.4 min** |
| `21ca97c0` | gemma→timeout → **qwen✓** | **4.1 min** |
| `0cb06206` | gemma→timeout → **Kimi✓** | **56.6s** |
| `66f5e9e5` | gemma→timeout → **GLM-5✓** | **80.6s** |

### 4. Gateway can't even spawn subagents

Attempting `sessions_spawn` returns:
```
gateway timeout after 10000ms
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789
```

Meanwhile, direct curl to the same endpoints returns in <1s.

### 5. "Overloaded" misclassification

The gateway logs show `reason: "overloaded"` with `errorPreview: "Live session model switch requested: novita/zai-org/glm-4.7"`. A **session model mismatch** is being classified as a provider overload — the gateway is conflating an internal session state error with provider unavailability.

## Expected Behaviour

- Requests to healthy, sub-second local endpoints should not time out
- Session model 

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

Comments/context:
```markdown
- steipete at 2026-04-27T11:28:45Z:
Partially addressed the concrete misclassification in 3da4b28d1b; leaving this open for the independent timeout/unresponsive-gateway symptoms.

What changed:
- `LiveSessionModelSwitchError` is no longer converted to `reason: "overloaded"` in model fallback.
- Stale live-session switch conflicts still become failover errors so outer retry loops do not restart forever, but they now record `reason: "unknown"` instead of applying overloaded cooldown semantics to the provider.
- Redirect-to-later-candidate behavior is unchanged when the requested live-session model matches a later fallback candidate.

Verification:
- `pnpm test src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- post-rebase `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`

I did not close this because the report also includes direct timeout and `sessions_spawn` latency symptoms; those need separate reproduction or logs after this classification fix.

Covers: - Linux allowlist path/glob contract tests - safeBins profile/trusted-dir/argv contract tests - effective policy merge tests - Clay Blender/script command-contract hardening Linear: LN3-170, LN3-175 ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-07T10:19:17Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR lands the Exec V2 contract follow-through: new test coverage for Linux allowlist path/glob contracts, `safeBins` profile/trusted-dir/argv semantics, effective policy merge, and Blender/Python script command-contract hardening. The core production changes add `cat` and `ls` as rejected safe bins, tighten `safeBinTrustedDirs` to silently drop mutable paths (`/usr/local/bin`, `/snap/bin`, home-scoped dirs), and introduce `resolveInterpreterScriptCandidate` so `blender --python script.py` / `python3 script.py` allowlist matching targets the script path rather than the interpreter binary. - The relative-path filtering in `resolveTrustedSafeBinDirs` has a correctness gap: `classifyRiskyExplicitSafeBinTrustedDir` is invoked after `path.resolve()`, so `./scripts` in a non-home-dir CWD is not caught by the relative-path guard and passes through as a trusted dir. The test covering this case is CWD-dependent and would fail on CI agents running outside `/home/`.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Mostly safe to merge; the relative-path trusted-dir enforcement gap should be fixed before landing. A P1 correctness gap exists in `resolveTrustedSafeBinDirs`: relative config paths that resolve outside `/home/`-family dirs bypass the new mutable-dir filter, and the test for this is CWD-dependent. input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 72133
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/72133
- Title: Feature request: per-message token/cost metadata in mobile app and channel surfaces
- State: OPEN
- Author: jarvisaibowen
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, impact:session-state, issue-rating: 🌊 off-meta tidepool

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

The Control UI (`localhost:18789`) already shows excellent per-message token/cost metadata beneath each assistant reply:

```
↑59 ↓13.4k R2.2M W43.9k 0% ctx claude-sonnet-4-6
```

This shows input tokens, output tokens, cache read/write, context window %, and model — and it turns **yellow at 75% ctx** and **red at 90% ctx** as a warning. It is genuinely excellent.

## Request

Surface this same data (or a condensed version) in:

1. **iOS/Android native chat view** — even a subtle footer line per assistant message would be valuable
2. **Channel surfaces** (Signal, iMessage, Telegram, BlueBubbles, etc.) — as an optional footer appended to replies, ideally behind a user toggle/setting

## Use Case

Users actively chatting via mobile or messaging channels have no visibility into context fill. They only discover the session is large when responses slow down or costs spike. Knowing the `% ctx` in real-time lets users decide when to `/compact` or start a fresh session — preventing runaway costs.

The Control UI implementation is already perfect. This is purely a request to extend that visibility to the surfaces where many users spend most of their time.

## Workarounds Considered
- Portal widget polling `session_status` — works but not per-message
- Userscript injection — fragile across browser updates
- Native support would be far superior

Thanks for considering this!
```

- Maintainers can also comment `@clawsweeper review` to request a fresh review only.
- Fresh-review commands do not start repair, autofix, rebase, CI repair, or automerge.
- Maintainer-only repair and merge flows require explicit commands such as `@clawsweeper autofix`, `@clawsweeper automerge`, `@clawsweeper fix ci`, or `@clawsweeper address review`.
- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #10467: [Feature Request]: Multi-lane concurrency support for sub-agents via sessions_spawn
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 10467
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10467
- Title: [Feature Request]: Multi-lane concurrency support for sub-agents via sessions_spawn
- State: OPEN
- Author: lexobe
- Labels: enhancement, P2, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Currently, all sub-agents spawned via the `sessions_spawn` tool are funneled into a single global queue lane: `subagent`. The concurrency is governed by a single limit `agents.defaults.subagents.maxConcurrent`.

This creates a bottleneck and reliability risk for complex multi-agent workflows (e.g., 'Evolution Engineering' patterns) where multiple independent paths of reasoning or tool usage are required.

## Problem

1. **Lane Exhaustion**: A burst of slow 'research' sub-agents can fill the `subagent` lane, blocking high-priority 'monitoring' or 'security' sub-agents.
2. **Lock Contention**: As noted in issue #4355, concurrent sub-agents often collide on session file locks. Named lanes would allow grouping related tasks to better manage these risks.
3. **Implicit Serialization**: Agents cannot currently specify parallel 'lanes' for different logic branches, forcing a flatter, more congested execution model.

## Proposed Solution

Add an optional `lane` parameter to the `sessions_spawn` tool.

```javascript
// Example usage in an agent turn
sessions_spawn(task: "Research market trends", lane: "research")
sessions_spawn(task: "Monitor security logs", lane: "security")
```

The OpenClaw gateway should:
1. Treat these as independent queue lanes.
2. Allow configuring `maxConcurrent` per lane in `openclaw.json`.
3. Fall back to the default `subagent` lane if no lane is specified.

## Benefits

- **Isolation**: Prevent low-priority background tasks from starving critical infrastructure sub-agents.
- **Scalability**: Support true 'multi-path' concurrent reasoning where an agent can spin up independent research and execution lanes.
- **Reliability**: Reduce cascading failures caused by a single slow task type saturating the global sub-agent pool.

## Environment
- OpenClaw version: 2026.2.3-1
- Use case: Multi-path autonomous evolution (Phase 110+ synthesis)
```

Comments/context:
```markdown
- jondecker76 at 2026-03-06T21:09:18Z:
+1  The single lane issue has made OpenClaw unusable for anything reasonably complex

- steipete at 2026-04-26T00:28:39Z:
Codex review: keeping this open for maintainer follow-up; there is still a little grit to resolve.

Keep this issue open. Current main still implements `sessions_spawn` with one fixed `subagent` queue lane: the tool schema has no `lane` parameter, native and ACP spawn paths both dispatch with `AGENT_LANE_SUBAGENT`, config/schema still expose only the scalar `agents.defaults.subagents.maxConcurrent`, and docs/tests explicitly describe/assert the single global lane. The maintainer-side discussion from April 26, 2026 remains accurate against current main.

Required change / next step:

This is a concrete, narrow feature gap with clear implementation files and acceptance tests; it does not require closing the issue first or a broad product decision beyond preserving existing defaults.

Best possible solution:

Add an additive `sessions_spawn.lane` parameter that defaults to `subagent`, validates lane names, forwards the resolved lane through both native and ACP spawn paths, applies compatible per-lane subagent concurrency configuration, and updates schema, docs, and regression tests without changing existing callers.

Acceptance criteria:

- pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts
- pnpm test src/config/config.

[comments/context truncated after 1500 chars]
```
input.ds4_description: Feature request to add multi-lane concurrency support for sub-agents spawned via sessions_spawn, enabling per-lane maxConcurrent configuration in openclaw.json to prevent lane exhaustion and lock contention in complex multi-agent workflows.

Body:
```markdown
### Bug type

Crash (process/app exits or hangs)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

# Bug: Memory provider cutover to Ollama leaves production index in mixed OpenAI/Ollama vector state after live sync/reload

## Summary

Switching `agents.defaults.memorySearch` from OpenAI embeddings to Ollama embeddings can leave the live production memory index in an inconsistent state after the provider change is loaded.

In my repro, a clean isolated Ollama index completed successfully with 768-dimensional vectors, but after copying/cutting over to the production memory path and loading the config change, `openclaw memory status --deep` reported:

- `Provider: ollama (requested: ollama)`
- `Model: nomic-embed-text:latest`
- `Indexed: 582/582 files · 6474 chunks`
- `Dirty: no`
- `Vector dims: 1536`

That is inconsistent: `nomic-embed-text` via Ollama produced 768-dimensional vectors in the isolated build, while 1536 dimensions matches the previous OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` index.

A known-good canary search then returned no matches. Rolling back the config and SQLite index to the prior OpenAI backup restored canary search results.

This appears related to the memory provider-change / live sync / auto-reindex path, rather than the initial Ollama embedding build itself.

## Environment

- OpenClaw version: `2026.5.7`
- OS/runtime: Linux container, Node `24.14.0`
- Install/runtime style: npm/global OpenClaw CLI inside container
- Memory engine: built-in SQLite memory search (`memory-core` plugin)
- Previous provider/model: `openai` / `text-embedding-3-small` / 1536 dims
- New provider/model: `ollama` / `nomic-embed-text:latest` / 768 dims
- Ollama endpoint: configured local/container Ollama provider

## Configuration change

Production config was changed under:

```json5
agents.defaults.memorySearch = {
  enabled: true,
  provider: "ollama",
  model: "nomic-embed-text:latest",
  fallback: "none",
  sync: {
    embeddingBatchTimeoutSeconds: 600
  }
}
```

The setting location matches the docs: `agents.defaults.memorySearch`.





After cutover/reload, production memory reported an inconsistent state:

- Provider/model: Ollama / `nomic-embed-text:latest`
- Vector dims: `1536`, matching previous OpenAI index rather than Ollama's 768-dim isolated index
- Dirty: `no`
- Canary search: no matches

Rollback to the OpenAI backup restored search.

## Why this may be related to #60856 but distinct

This is adjacent to #60856 because it involves Ollama-backed memory sea

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

input.github_context:

- Title: test(acpx): accept built-dist MCP server resolution when dist exists
- State: OPEN
- Author: KhanCold
- Labels: size: XS, extensions: acpx, proof: supplied, proof: sufficient
- Changed file count available to wrapper: 1
- Changed files: extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts

Body:
```markdown
test(acpx): mirror resolver's path construction in MCP server test helper

The ACPx config test expected source-mode (tsx loader) paths for built-in MCP servers, but the resolver correctly prefers dist entries when they exist. Update the test helper to match this runtime behavior.

- Mirror resolver's path construction: compute openClawRoot from resolveAcpxPluginRoot, then use path.join/path.relative to build the correct absolute dist path.
- Remove the broken entrypoint.replace("/src/", "/dist/") which never matched because the test passes "src/mcp/..." without a leading separator.

Fixes #80431

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The test helper `expectedMcpServerArgs` used `entrypoint.replace("/src/", "/dist/")` which never matched because the caller passes "src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.ts" (no leading `/`). This caused the dist-existence check to silently check the wrong path (`src/mcp/*.js` instead of `dist/mcp/*.js`) and always fall back to source-mode args, even when `dist/` existed.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Ubuntu 22.04, Node 20, pnpm 9. Built dist exists for built-in MCP servers under `dist/mcp/`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
cd /tmp/openclaw-fork
git checkout fix/issue-80431-acpx-dist-path
pnpm build
ls -la dist/mcp/  # confirm dist exists
npx vitest run extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**
Terminal output showing the corrected test helper now constructs the correct absolute dist path:
```
$ npx vitest run extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts
 PASS  extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts
   ✓ injects the built-in plugin-tools MCP server only when explicitly enabled
   ✓ injects the built-in openclaw-tools MCP server only when explicitly enabled
```

Before the fix, the helper computed `distEntry` as `src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.js` (because `/src/` did not match) and `fs.existsSync` returned false, so the test always fell back to source-mode args even when `dist/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.js` existed.

**Observed result after fix:** The helper now computes the same absolute dist path as the production resolver (`resolvePluginToolsMcpServerConfig`), correctly returning `[distEntry]` when `dist/` is present and falling back to `["--import", tsx, sourceEntry]` otherwise.

**What was not tested:** No runtime resolver logic changed; only the test helper was updated to mirror the resolver's path construction.
```

From cafcde00284fe711db71a895f971bbce81b188d8 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: KhanCold <shiqiming2000@gmail.com>
Date: Mon, 11 May 2026 11:12:59 +0800
Subject: [PATCH 2/2] test(acpx): mirror resolver's path construction in MCP
 server test helper

The previous helper used entrypoint.replace("/src/", "/dist/") which
never matched because the test passes "src/mcp/..." without a leading
separator. This caused the dist-existence check to silently check the
wrong path and always fall back to source-mode args.

Fix by computing openClawRoot from resolveAcpxPluginRoot (matching the
production resolver) and using path.join/path.relative to construct the
correct absolute dist path. This mirrors resolvePluginToolsMcpServerConfig
and resolveOpenClawToolsMcpServerConfig in config.ts.
---

diff --git a/extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts b/extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts
index d3772c7cb862..9f6dfd3aa0e5 100644
--- a/extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts
+++ b/extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts
@@ -8,11 +8,21 @@ const requireFromTest = createRequire(import.meta.url);
 const TSX_IMPORT = requireFromTest.resolve("tsx");
 
 function expectedMcpServerArgs(entrypoint: string): string[] {
-  const distEntry = entrypoint.replace("/src/", "/dist/").replace(/\.ts$/, ".js");
+  const pluginRoot = resolveAcpxPluginRoot(import.meta.url);
+  const parent = path.dirname(pluginRoot);
+  const openClawRoot = path.basename(parent) === "extensions"

+  const sourceEntry = path.join(openClawRoot, entrypoint);
+  const distEntry = path.join(

+    "dist",
+    path.relative(path.join(openClawRoot, "src"), sourceEntry)
+  ).replace(/\.ts$/, ".js");
   if (fs.existsSync(distEntry)) {
     return [distEntry];
   }
-  return ["--import", TSX_IMPORT, path.resolve(entrypoint)];
+  return ["--import", TSX_IMPORT, sourceEntry];
 }

input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: pull_request
- Number: 84668
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84668
- Title: docs(agent-runtimes): clarify model name vs runtime routing for Codex (#84637)
- State: OPEN
- Author: hclsys
- Labels: docs, size: XS, proof: supplied, P3, rating: 🐚 platinum hermit, status: 👀 ready for maintainer look
- Changed file count available to wrapper: 1
- Changed files: docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md

Body:
```markdown
Fixes #84637 (docs portion, item #4 in the issue's "Requested fix" list).

The issue reports an incident where an agent told to "remove Codex runtime from normal sessions" repeatedly removed `openai/gpt-5.3-codex` from fallbacks instead — three times across one morning — because the model ID contains the substring `codex`. `openai/gpt-5.3-codex` was actually a valid Pi-routed fallback (model-scoped `agentRuntime.id: "pi"`); removing it was the wrong fix.

The issue lists four requested guardrails. **#1-#3 are doctor/status enhancements (separate scope)**; **#4 is documentation**: "Documentation should explicitly say: Codex runtime/harness is not the same thing as `gpt-*-codex` model IDs."

This PR ships item #4. `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md` already discusses runtime-vs-provider layering and has a "Codex surfaces" section enumerating four Codex-named concepts (app-server runtime, OAuth profile, ACP adapter, chat command). It does NOT, however, explicitly call out the **model name** with substring `codex` as a fifth confusable surface that is name-only, not runtime evidence. The Warning added here closes that specific gap.

## Changes

- `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md`: add a `<Warning>` block immediately after the "Codex surfaces" table that:
  - States the rule: a model name containing `codex` is NOT runtime evidence
  - Cites the exact case from the issue: `openai/gpt-5.3-codex` as a valid Pi-routed fallback
  - Distinguishes runtime routing (`agentRuntime.id`) from model selection (the model ref / fallback list) on separate bulleted lines
  - Provides concrete audit guidance keyed off `agentRuntime.id` (the authoritative field), with model-name and stale-label exclusions
  - References #84637 so future operators searching for the symptom find the rule

Diff stat: 1 file, +25 / -0. Documentation-only; bypasses the real-CLI-invocation proof gate (`docs/...` files only).

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Sanitized issue evidence — the issue's "Requested fix" section item #4 explicitly names "Documentation should explicitly say: Codex runtime/harness is not the same thing as `gpt-*-codex` model IDs." The Warning lands in `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md` (the canonical agent-runtimes concept doc that already enumerates the Codex confusion surfaces) at the section that already handles the topic.

- **Real environment tested:** Local Node 22.x. Probe at `/tmp/probe_84637.mjs` does structural verification: (a) the `<Warning>` is

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

Diff/context:
```diff
From be03d1b0700e99670d6d2112c8ecedef53e9b9a6 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: HCL <chenglunhu@gmail.com>
Date: Thu, 21 May 2026 02:39:16 +0800
Subject: [PATCH] docs(agent-runtimes): clarify model name vs runtime routing
 for Codex (#84637)
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=UTF-8
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 8bit

Operators repeatedly conflated 'Codex runtime' (the embedded agent
loop, `agentRuntime.id = "codex"`) with 'Codex-named models' such as
`openai/gpt-5.3-codex` (model selection, agnostic to runtime). The
reported incident: an agent told to remove Codex runtime kept removing
`openai/gpt-5.3-codex` from fallbacks instead, three times across one
morning — because the model ID contained the substring `codex`.

Add a <Warning> in the 'Codex surfaces' section of
docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md that:
- States the rule (model name with 'codex' is NOT runtime evidence)
- Cites the exact problem case (`openai/gpt-5.3-codex` as a valid
  fallback for a Pi-routed agent)
- Gives concrete audit guidance keyed off `agentRuntime.id`
- Calls out stale `systemPromptReport.provider` labels as separate
  from active runtime routing
- References #84637 so future operators searching for the symptom find
  the rule.
---
 docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md | 25 +++++++++++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 25 insertions(+)

diff --git a/docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md b/docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md
index bd12cafac939..1f808d272393 100644
--- a/docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md
+++ b/docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md
@@ -58,6 +58,31 @@ the native app-server features available; `openclaw doctor --fix` owns legacy
 `openai/*` for an agent model now means "run this through Codex" unless a
 non-agent OpenAI API surface is being used.
 
+<Warning>
+**A model name that contains the substring `codex` is NOT runtime evidence.**
+Specifically, model IDs such as `openai/gpt-5.3-codex` or `openai/gpt-5.5-codex`
+are *model selections*, not *runtime selections*. They can be valid fallbacks
+for a Pi-routed agent — the model is allowed to run through Pi runtime when its
+`agentRuntime.id` resolves to `pi`. Removing a `gpt-*-codex` model from
+`fallbacks` because its ID contains `codex` is the wrong fix when the goal is
+to stop using Codex *runtime*. The two layers must be evaluated independently:
+
+- **Runtime routing** lives in `agentRuntime.id` (`pi`, `codex`, `claude-cli`, …) on the provider/model entry.
+- **Model selection** lives in the model ref (`openai/gpt-5.5`, `openai/gpt-5.3-codex`, `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, …) and in `fallbacks`.
+
+To audit "no Codex _runtime_ in normal sessions" without touching fallback policy:
+
+- inspect resolved `agentRuntime.id` on every provider/model entry and session row — only those that equal `"codex"` are evidence;
+- model IDs whose name contains `codex` are NOT evidence by themselves;
+- stale `systemPromptReport.provider` labels left over from earlier sessions are NOT evidence — they describe the prior route, not the current one.
+
+The shared substring `codex` across provider keys, auth labels, model IDs, the
+embedded harness, the ACP adapter, and the `/codex` chat command is real but
+unavoidable. When in doubt, route every claim through `agentRuntime.id` rather
+than name-matching. See #84637 for an incident where this distinction was
+missed in production.
+</Warning>
+
 The common ChatGPT/Codex subscription setup uses Codex OAuth for auth, but keeps
 the model ref as `openai/*` and selects the `codex` runtime:
 

```
input.ds4_description: Docs-only PR adding a Warning block to docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md clarifying that Codex-named model IDs (e.g., openai/gpt-5.3-codex) are model selections, not runtime evidence; runtime routing is determined by agentRuntime.id, not the model name. Addresses item #4 from issue #84637.
input.title: docs(agent-runtimes): clarify model name vs runtime routing for Codex (#84637)

input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #69260: Harden Gemini ACP integration against ambient API-key fallback and add generic auth-contract hooks for ACP agents
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 69260
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/69260
- Title: Harden Gemini ACP integration against ambient API-key fallback and add generic auth-contract hooks for ACP agents
- State: OPEN
- Author: anyech
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, clawsweeper:linked-pr-open, impact:security, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
### Summary
This is an integration-hardening / defense-in-depth issue, not a claim that OpenClaw is the sole root cause of Gemini auth drift.

We just finished a local incident/debugging cycle where a risky Gemini ACP path could drift durable Gemini auth away from `oauth-personal` and into the API-key lane when `GEMINI_API_KEY` was ambient.

The deepest bug seam appears to belong upstream in Gemini CLI, because ACP auth handling seems able to persist a fallback auth method back into durable Gemini settings.

However, OpenClaw also contributed to the exposure: the ACP launch/runtime path allowed provider auth env to be inherited broadly. That is a defensible default in some environments, but in a mixed-credential host it makes the wrong auth lane eligible unless the integration hardens against it explicitly.

### What local stage validation showed
We ran an isolated-HOME comparison without touching live user state.

#### Old risky path
- no explicit outer auth selector for the Gemini ACP lane
- bare Gemini ACP launch path
- ambient `GEMINI_API_KEY`

Result:
- the staged auth state drifted to the API-key lane

#### Hardened local path
- Gemini durable settings pinned to `oauth-personal`
- explicit outer selection guard for the Gemini ACP lane
- Gemini launch path strips Gemini / GenAI auth env before exec
- gateway/runtime no longer carries broad Gemini auth env by default

Result:
- the same staged pressure did not drift the auth state back

### Why file this in OpenClaw
Even if the deepest fix lands upstream in Gemini CLI, OpenClaw still has useful product-level hardening work here.

The lesson is broader than Gemini:

> ACP-backed agent integrations that depend on a specific auth mode should be able to declare that auth contract explicitly, scrub incompatible ambient auth env, and fail closed when the contract is not met.

### Suggested product direction
#### 1. Auth-contract metadata on ACP agent definitions
Allow an ACP-backed agent or route to declare:
- expected auth mode
- whether fallback auth methods are allowed
- whether ambient provider auth env should be stripped

#### 2. Integration-level env scrubbing hooks
Provide a first-class way to remove provider auth env from ACP launches when a route is supposed to use another auth surface.

For example, Gemini ACP routes should be able to scrub things like:
- `GEMINI_API_KEY`
- `GOOGLE_API_KEY`
- `GOOGLE_GENAI_API_KEY`
- `GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI`
- `GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_GCA`

#### 3. Fail-closed prefligh

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #68725: feat(amazon-bedrock-mantle): add known context windows for open-weight Mantle models
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: pull_request
- Number: 68725
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/68725
- Title: feat(amazon-bedrock-mantle): add known context windows for open-weight Mantle models
- State: OPEN
- Author: wirjo
- Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, status: 📣 needs proof

Body:
```markdown
# feat(amazon-bedrock-mantle): add known context windows for open-weight Mantle models

## Problem

Mantle's `/v1/models` endpoint returns only model IDs — no token limit metadata. Discovery hardcodes `contextWindow: 32000` for every model, which is wrong for most:

- MiniMax M2/M2.1: **1,000,000** (gets 32K)
- Qwen3 Coder: **256,000** (gets 32K)
- DeepSeek V3.x, GLM 4.x, Nemotron: **128,000** (gets 32K)

This causes the same premature context overflow and wrong compaction thresholds as #65952 (the amazon-bedrock equivalent).

## Fix

Add a `KNOWN_CONTEXT_WINDOWS` lookup table for open-weight models currently available on Mantle. Raise the default fallback from 32K → 128K for unknown models.

```
MiniMax M2 / M2.1              → 1,000,000
Qwen3 Coder (480B/Next/30B)    → 256,000
Qwen3 235B / 32B               → 128,000
DeepSeek V3.1 / V3.2           → 128,000
GLM 4.6 / 4.7 / 4.7 Flash      → 128,000
NVIDIA Nemotron (all variants)  → 128,000
```

**Claude models are intentionally excluded** — they use a separate Anthropic-native endpoint on Mantle (`/anthropic/v1/messages`), not the OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` endpoint that this extension handles. Claude model metadata is managed by the `amazon-bedrock` extension (#65952).

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/discovery.ts` | +41/-2 — context window map, `resolveKnownContextWindow()`, default bump |

Single file, no type system changes.

## References

- [Mantle model list](https://conoroneill.net/2026/02/20/aws-bedrock-mantle-model-names-youve-been-looking-for/) (community, Feb 2026)
- [Opus 4.7 model card](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-card-anthropic-claude-opus-4-7.html) — confirms Claude uses `/anthropic/v1/messages` on Mantle, not Chat Completions
- Related: #65952 (same pattern for amazon-bedrock discovery)

Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, status: 📣 needs proof
```
input.ds4_description: Adds known context windows for open-weight Mantle models (MiniMax, Qwen, DeepSeek, GLM, Nemotron) in the amazon-bedrock-mantle extension, fixing premature context overflow by replacing a hardcoded 32K default with per-model token limits.
ds4_caveats: Context is from PR description only; no diff or comments reviewed.
input.title: feat(amazon-bedrock-mantle): add known context windows for open-weight Mantle models

Today they must parse JSONL transcripts after the fact. This change surfaces the data directly in the `agent.wait` WebSocket response, which is the natural integration point for synchronous orchestration flows. Paperclip's adapter (`@paperclipai/adapter-openclaw-gateway`) already has `parseUsage()` ready to consume `usage`, `costUsd`, `provider`, and `model` from response metadata — it just gets `undefined` today. The `meta` field is only included in `agent.wait` responses to authenticated gateway callers (trusted operators per the security policy). No secrets, credentials, or sensitive data are exposed — only token counts and estimated cost. ## Test plan - [ ] Existing `agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts` tests pass (backward-compatible) - [ ] `meta` appears in `agent.wait` response when a run completes with usage data - [ ] `meta` is omitted when no agent meta is available (error paths) - [ ] `costUsd` is populated when model cost config exists, omitted otherwise 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com [body truncated after 2500 chars] ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-18T05:45:25Z:

input.github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: pull_request - Number: 63826 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/63826 - Title: security: fix HIGH/CRITICAL vulns in skill scanner, SSRF, hook priority, and token verification - State: OPEN - Author: hawonb711-tech - Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider, merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary, status: 📣 needs proof - Changed file count available to wrapper: 3 - Changed files: src/plugins/provider-self-hosted-setup.ts, src/plugins/registry.ts, src/security/skill-scanner.ts Body: ```markdown ## Summary Security audit identified 4 HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities. This PR fixes all of them with minimal, targeted changes. ### 1. Skill Scanner Bypass (CRITICAL) — `src/security/skill-scanner.ts` All 9 existing scanner rules are bypassable via basic JavaScript obfuscation (dynamic property access, import aliasing, prototype pollution, indirect eval). **Fix:** Add 6 new LINE_RULES and 3 new SOURCE_RULES: - `dangerous-exec-dynamic`: detects `cp["exec"]()` bracket notation - `dangerous-exec-indirect`: detects `Reflect.apply`, `Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor` - `dynamic-code-execution-indirect`: detects `globalThis[...]`, `.constructor.constructor` - `prototype-pollution`: detects `__proto__`, `Object.prototype.x =` - `import-aliasing`: detects `{ exec: aliasName }` destructuring - Unicode escape sequences, `String.fromCharCode`, dynamic `import()` of dangerous modules - Lower base64 threshold from 200→100 chars, hex threshold from 6→4 sequences - Expand network API coverage: `http.get`, `XMLHttpRequest`, `WebSocket`, `axios` ### 2. SSRF via Unguarded fetch() (HIGH) — `src/plugins/provider-self-hosted-setup.ts` `discoverOpenAICompatibleLocalModels()` uses raw `fetch()` with user-provided `baseUrl`, enabling SSRF to cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254), internal services, and API key leakage. **Fix:** Replace `fetch()` with `fetchWithSsrFGuard()` using `allowPrivateNetwork: true` (needed for legitimate local providers) and proper dispatcher release. ### 3. Hook Priority TOCTOU (CRITICAL) — `src/plugins/registry.ts` No upper bound on hook `priority` allows malicious plugins to register at priority 9999, running before security-critical hooks to auto-approve dangerous tool calls, inject prompts, or tamper transcripts. **Fix:** Cap priority for external plugins at 100 and bundled plugins at 1000. Log diagnostic warning when priority is clamped. ### 4. Device Token Revocation TOCTOU (HIGH) — `src/infra/device-pairing.ts` `verifyDeviceToken()` checks `revokedAtMs` early but mutates state later. A concurrent `revokeDeviceToken()` can revoke the token between check and state mutation. **Fix:** Re-check `revokedAtMs` immediately before `lastUsedAtMs` update to narrow the TOCTOU window. ## PoC Documents Detailed proof-of-concept documents with exploit code are included in `security-pocs/`: - `01-skill-scanner-bypass.md` — 5 complete bypass techniques - `02-ssrf-unguarded-fetch.md` — 4 SSRF attack scenarios - `03-hook-priority-toctou.md` — 4 hook [body truncated after 2500 chars] ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-09T16:07:55Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR addresses four reported security issues: skill-scanner bypass, SSRF via unguarded `fetch()`, hook priority manipulation, and a device-token revocation race. The scanner and hook-priority changes are solid; the SSRF and TOCTOU fixes each have a correctness problem that needs attention before merge. - **P1 — dispatcher leak** (`src/plugins/provider-self-hosted-setup.ts`): `release` is scoped inside the `try` block; the `catch` handler cannot call it, so the undici dispatcher leaks if `response.json()` throws. A `finally` block (or hoisting `release` before the `try`) is needed. - **P1 — TOCTOU fix is a no-op** (`src/infra/device-pairing.ts`): both `revokedAtMs` checks sit inside the same `withLock()` closure — the mutex already prevents any concurrent mutation between them. The actual race is external (caller acts on `{ ok: true }` after the lock releases); the patch does not address it.

Confidence Score: 3/5

Two P1 defects — a dispatcher resource leak and a no-op TOCTOU mitigation — should be resolved before merging. The hook-priority cap and scanner rule additions are correct and well-constructed. However, the SSRF fix has a real dispatcher leak in the exception path, and the device-pairing TOCTOU patch doesn't actually reduce the race window it claims to fix. Both are present defects on the changed path. src/plugins/provider-self-hosted-setup.ts (release() leak), src/infra/device-pairin [comments/context truncated after 1500 chars] ``` Diff/context: ++++++++++++++++++++++ security-pocs/04-device-token-toctou.md | 74 ++++++++++++++ src/plugins/provider-self-hosted-setup.ts | 17 +++- src/security/skill-scanner.ts | 63 ++++++++++-- create mode 100644 security-pocs/01-skill-scanner-bypass.md create mode 100644 security-pocs/02-ssrf-unguarded-fetch.md create mode 100644 security-pocs/03-hook-priority-toctou.md create mode 100644 security-pocs/04-device-token-toctou.md diff --git a/security-pocs/01-skill-scanner-bypass.md b/security-pocs/01-skill-scanner-bypass.md --- /dev/null +++ b/security-pocs/01-skill-scanner-bypass.md @@ -0,0 +1,110 @@ +`src/security/skill-scanner.ts` uses regex-only pattern matching for all 9 rules. +All while passing the "security scan" with zero findings. diff --git Greptile notes a dispatcher leak in the SSRF fix and that the TOCTOU fix is a no-op under the existing mutex.
ds4_caveats: Greptile identified two P1 defects: SSRF fix has a dispatcher leak (release() scoped inside try, unreachable in catch), and device token TOCTOU fix is a no-op (both checks are inside the same mutex closure). Skill scanner rules and hook priority cap are considered correct.

input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #80431: ACPx plugin-tools MCP config test expects source path but resolver returns dist path
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 80431
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80431
- Title: ACPx plugin-tools MCP config test expects source path but resolver returns dist path
- State: OPEN
- Author: 100yenadmin

Body:
```markdown
# TLDR

`pnpm test` currently fails one ACPx config test because the resolved built-in plugin-tools MCP server points at the built `dist` server, while the test expects the source `tsx` loader path.

**Priority if OpenClaw moved fully to Codex today: P4 product impact, P1/P2 test-suite hygiene depending on whether source-mode behavior is still required.** This is not a Codex parity harness bug.

# What Is Wrong

The test `injects the built-in plugin-tools MCP server only when explicitly enabled` expects:

```text
node --import <repo>/node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs <repo>/src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.ts
```

The runtime resolved:

```text
node <repo>/dist/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.js
```

That may be a correct built-runtime behavior, but it means the test contract is no longer aligned with the resolver output in this environment.

# Evidence

Command:

```bash
pnpm test
```

Observed:

```text
FAIL |extension-acpx| extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts > embedded acpx plugin config > injects the built-in plugin-tools MCP server only when explicitly enabled
Expected args: ["--import", ".../node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs", ".../src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.ts"]
Received args: [".../dist/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.js"]
```

The affected files are not touched by PR #80323:

```bash
git diff --name-only upstream/main...HEAD | rg '^extensions/acpx' || true
# no output
```

# Expected Fix

Clarify whether tests should force source-mode resolution or accept built-dist resolution when `dist` exists. Then update the test/helper accordingly without weakening the assertion that plugin-tools MCP is injected only when explicitly enabled.

# Related Context

Found during the full local rerun for #80323; track separately from Codex-vs-Pi runtime parity.

```

```markdown
- clawsweeper at 2026-05-10T21:33:58Z:
Codex review: keeping this open for maintainer follow-up; there is still a little grit to resolve.

**Summary**
Keep open: current main still has an environment-sensitive ACPX test mismatch where the resolver prefers a built MCP server when `dist` exists but the test always expects the source `tsx` entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection plus the reported failure show the resolver returns the dist MCP entry when it exists while the test expects the source entry unconditionally.

**Next step**
This is a narrow, source-provable ACPX test mismatch with clear files and validation, and it does not require a new product or config decision if runtime behavior stays unchanged.


<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Keep the existing resolver contract and make the ACPX config test hermetic for both dist-present and source-fallback paths while preserving the explicit opt-in bridge assertion.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes. Source inspection plus the reported failure show the resolver returns the dist MCP entry when it exists while the test expects the source entry unconditionally.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

Yes. The narrow maintainable fix is to update the ACPX config test/helper to reflect or fixture the resolver's dist-first behavior, not to weaken the bridge enablement checks.

Acceptance criteria:

- pnpm test extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts
- pnpm test

[comments/context truncated after 1500 chars]
```

input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #39248: Bug: sandbox.mode: "non-main" silently breaks sessions_spawn subagent initialization
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 39248
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/39248
- Title: Bug: sandbox.mode: "non-main" silently breaks sessions_spawn subagent initialization
- State: OPEN
- Author: alexputici
- Labels: P1, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-live-repro, impact:session-state, impact:crash-loop, issue-rating: 🐚 platinum hermit

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

When `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode` is set to `"non-main"`, sub-agents spawned via `sessions_spawn` fail to initialize. Sessions are accepted (return valid `childSessionKey` and `runId`) but never start executing — zero tokens, zero messages, no transcript files created. **No errors appear in gateway logs.**

## Environment

- OpenClaw Version: 2026.3.2 (85377a2)
- OS: macOS 15.3.0 (Apple Silicon, arm64)
- Node: v25.8.0
- Docker Desktop: running (coding agent sandbox works fine)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode: "non-main"` in config
2. Restart gateway
3. From main agent, spawn a sub-agent:
   ```
   sessions_spawn(task="Reply with: test", model="sonnet")
   ```
4. Check `sessions_history` or `subagents list` after 30+ seconds

## Expected Behavior

Sub-agent starts within seconds, `systemSent: true`, tokens accumulate, task completes.

## Actual Behavior

- `sessions_spawn` returns `status: "accepted"` with valid session key
- Sub-agent appears in `subagents list` as `running`
- `sessions_history` returns `messages: []` indefinitely
- No transcript file created on disk
- `systemSent` never set to `true`
- `totalTokens` never set
- **Zero error output in gateway logs**
- Eventually times out via `runTimeoutSeconds`

## Bisect Results

Systematically tested with a simple one-line task (`Reply with exactly: ...`):

| Test | sandbox.mode | Docker settings | Result |
|------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| 1 | non-main | network: bridge, readOnlyRoot: true | stall |
| 2 | non-main | network: bridge, readOnlyRoot: false | stall |
| 3 | **off** | (same as test 2) | **works (4s)** |
| 4 | non-main | minimal (image + workdir only) | stall |

The issue is **not** caused by specific Docker settings (network, readOnlyRoot, tmpfs). Any `sandbox.mode` that enables Docker for subagents causes silent failure.

## Notes

- **Cron isolated sessions work fine** with `sandbox.mode: "non-main"` on the same config — they appear to use a different code path that bypasses container initialization.
- **Per-agent sandbox overrides are unaffected**: a coding agent with `sandbox.mode: "all"` and its own Docker settings works correctly.
- Possibly related to #5813 (similar symptoms, different attributed cause).
- The silent failure with zero log output makes this very difficult to diagnose.

## Workaround

Set `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode: "off"` and use per-agent `sandbox.mode: "all"` overrides for agents that specifically need san

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #84660: [Bug] Voice STT: empty moonshine transcripts passed as raw JSON to LLM, clogging serialized processing queue
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 84660
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84660
- Title: [Bug] Voice STT: empty moonshine transcripts passed as raw JSON to LLM, clogging serialized processing queue
- State: CLOSED
- Author: Joel-Claw
- Labels: P1, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:message-loss, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

When using moonshine-tiny-en for Discord voice STT, empty/noisy transcripts are passed as raw JSON strings to the LLM instead of being filtered out. This wastes ~4 seconds and ~24k input tokens per empty segment and clogs the serialized processing queue, making the bot appear unresponsive in voice.

## Reproduction

1. Configure OpenClaw with `voice.mode = "stt-tts"` and moonshine-tiny-en as the STT model
2. Join a voice channel with background noise or short utterances
3. Observe that short/noisy audio segments produce empty transcripts: `{"lang": "", "emotion": "", "event": "", "text": "", "timestamps": [], "durations": [], "tokens":[], "ys_log_probs": [], "words": []}`
4. These empty JSON strings are sent to the LLM as "transcripts" instead of being filtered
5. The LLM returns NO_REPLY (correct behavior), but each call wastes ~4s and ~24k tokens
6. The serialized processing queue (`entry.processingQueue`) blocks until each call completes
7. With ~35% of segments being empty JSON, the pipeline appears to "stop" responding

## Root Cause

In `manager.runtime`, `transcribeVoiceAudio()` calls `normalizeOptionalString()` on the STT result, which returns `undefined` for empty strings. However, the sherpa-onnx CLI output includes the entire JSON object on the last line, and the `mediaUnderstanding.transcribeAudioFile()` result appears to include the full JSON string as `text` even when the `"text"` field within it is empty.

The check at line ~1441 (`if (!transcript)`) catches `undefined` but NOT the full JSON string with an empty `"text"` field. So `{"text": "", ...}` passes through as a non-empty string transcript.

## Evidence

Session logs show:
```
Voice transcript from speaker "[CK] Alex the 'guin":
{"lang": "", "emotion": "", "event": "", "text": "", "timestamps": [], "durations": [], "tokens":[], "ys_log_probs": [], "words": []}
```

100% of NO_REPLY responses (8 out of 8 in a recent session) were triggered by these empty JSON transcripts. The bot responded correctly to all real transcripts but was blocked during empty JSON processing.

52 segment files accumulated in 10 minutes. Only 10 TTS outputs were generated. The pipeline was processing empty JSON ~35% of the time.

## Expected Behavior

1. When the STT model returns `"text": ""` (or equivalent empty transcript), the segment should be skipped entirely — no LLM call needed
2. The serialized processing queue should have a max depth or stale-segment discard mechanism to prevent pipeline 

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #44202: [Bug]: local memory embeddings on Apple Silicon can crash gateway in ggml-metal / node-llama-cpp; need official Metal/GPU guidance
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 44202
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/44202
- Title: [Bug]: local memory embeddings on Apple Silicon can crash gateway in ggml-metal / node-llama-cpp; need official Metal/GPU guidance
- State: OPEN
- Author: samersaibot
- Labels: P1, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, impact:crash-loop, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Local memory embeddings on macOS Apple Silicon can crash the gateway in the `node-llama-cpp` / `ggml-metal` path during restart/shutdown, even when the main chat model is healthy.

This report is being filed by `@samersaibot` on behalf of Samer Haddad after reproducing and recovering the issue on a Mac Studio. We are also asking for guidance on the ideal supported path for **GPU-backed local embeddings on Apple Silicon**, because the stable recovery we reached required disabling the Metal path for embeddings.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: `2026.3.11`
- Install method: global npm/homebrew-style install under `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw`
- OS: macOS Apple Silicon (Mac Studio, 64 GB RAM)
- Gateway service: LaunchAgent (`ai.openclaw.gateway`)
- Node seen by CLI: `v25.6.1`
- LaunchAgent runtime node: `/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin/node` (`v22.22.0`)
- Main model: `openai-codex/gpt-5.4`
- Memory provider: `local`
- Local embedding model: `hf:ggml-org/embeddinggemma-300m-qat-q8_0-GGUF/embeddinggemma-300m-qat-Q8_0.gguf`
- Vector store: sqlite-vec (`vec0.dylib`)

## What happened

1. Main model config was fixed successfully and the gateway itself was healthy.
2. With local memory embeddings enabled, the gateway repeatedly hit a native assertion in the local embedding runtime.
3. The crash was in `ggml-metal` / `node-llama-cpp`, not the main model path.
4. Temporary recovery was achieved by disabling memory search and reinstalling the LaunchAgent.
5. Local memory was later restored with two mitigations:
   - sequential local embed batch generation (to avoid known `Promise.all` deadlock risk, related to #7547)
   - forcing the local embedding runtime to CPU-only (disabling the Metal/GPU path for embeddings)
6. After that recovery, local memory became operational again:
   - indexed `25/25` files
   - `111` chunks
   - embeddings/vector/fts all `ready`

So the current system is usable again, but **the only stable local recovery we found was to avoid the Metal path for embeddings**.

## Actual crash evidence

From `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log`:

```text
/Users/runner/work/node-llama-cpp/node-llama-cpp/llama/llama.cpp/ggml/src/ggml-metal/ggml-metal-device.m:612: GGML_ASSERT([rsets->data count] == 0) failed
...
2   libggml-metal.so                    ... ggml_metal_device_init + 0
3   libggml-metal.so                    ... ggml_metal_device_free + 24
...
6   libsystem_c.dylib                   ... exit + 44
7   libnode.127.dylib             

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

```markdown
- samerGMTM22 at 2026-03-12T16:03:45Z:
If this is something others solved please help if a prevalent issue please address in next update

- Ryce at 2026-03-12T17:58:23Z:
Gateway crashes during restart/shutdown are exactly the kind of failure that makes external backup worth having. Even when the recovery path works, you're left guessing what state survived the crash and what didn't.

If you're running agents that do real work (code edits, file operations, scheduled tasks), an encrypted cloud backup gives you an independent checkpoint that survives Metal/GPU issues, gateway crashes, and any other local failure mode. You can verify what's actually backed up with a snapshot list after any restart, and restore to a known-good state if something got corrupted.

Keepmyclaw handles this for OpenClaw workspaces — client-side encrypted, scheduled, with restore drills you can test without risking live state. It's built on Cloudflare Workers/D1/R2 so it doesn't depend on the same local stack that just crashed.

- Ryce at 2026-03-12T20:43:42Z:
GGML Metal crashes taking down the gateway is a particularly painful failure mode because it can corrupt the in-memory state mid-operation. If this happens during a memory flush or workspace write, you end up with partially-written files or lost conversation context.

The recovery play here is the same pattern that protects against any gateway-level crash: external encrypted snapshots on a schedule that is independent of OpenClaw itself. If the Met

[comments/context truncated after 1500 chars]
```
